# Medical Diagnostic Retrieval System 

**Team:** Arunya, Srijan Reddy Sankepally
**Track:** A — RAG Pipeline | **Database:** PostgreSQL 16
**GitHub:** https://github.com/Arunyaiitm/medical-diagnostic-retrieval-system

---

## Data Source

**Primary dataset:** NIH Chest X-Ray Dataset (Sample) from Kaggle
- Link: https://www.kaggle.com/datasets/nih-chest-xrays/sample
- File: `sample_labels.csv` (5,606 records)
- License: Public domain (NIH Clinical Center)

**Supporting data:** Generated by `seed_data.py` with realistic medical constraints — doctor specialisations mapped to diagnosis types, chronological visit ordering, real medication-to-condition pairing. All logic documented in inline comments.

---

## Data Dictionary

### sample_labels.csv (NIH — real data)

| Column | Type | Description | Example |
|--------|------|-------------|---------|
| Image Index | string | X-ray image filename | 00000013_005.png |
| Finding Labels | string | Pipe-separated disease labels | Pneumonia\|Infiltration |
| Follow-up # | int | 0 = initial, 1+ = follow-up | 5 |
| Patient ID | int | Unique patient identifier | 13 |
| Patient Age | string | Age with unit suffix | 060Y (years), 013M (months) |
| Patient Gender | char | M or F | M |
| View Position | string | PA or AP | PA |
| OriginalImageWidth | int | Image width in pixels | 3056 |
| OriginalImageHeight | int | Image height in pixels | 2544 |
| OriginalImagePixelSpacing_x | float | mm per pixel | 0.139 |

### 14 disease labels
Atelectasis, Cardiomegaly, Consolidation, Edema, Effusion, Emphysema, Fibrosis, Hernia, Infiltration, Mass, Nodule, Pleural_Thickening, Pneumonia, Pneumothorax, No Finding

### Database tables after seeding

| Table | Rows | Source |
|-------|------|--------|
| departments | 5 | Seed script |
| doctors | 30 | Seed script |
| patients | 4,230 | NIH dataset |
| visits | 5,606 | NIH + seed |
| scans | 5,606 | NIH dataset |
| diagnoses | 6,978 | NIH dataset |
| prescriptions | 3,934 | Seed script |
| **Total** | **26,389** | |

### Key column details

**patients:** patient_id (INTEGER, from NIH), age (INTEGER, 0-130, parsed from formats like 060Y/013M), gender (CHAR, M/F), blood_group (VARCHAR, realistic distribution: A+/B+/O+/AB+ etc.)

**diagnoses:** finding_label (VARCHAR, one of 14 NIH labels + No Finding), severity (VARCHAR — MILD if 1 finding, MODERATE if 2, SEVERE if 3+), notes (TEXT, template-based clinical descriptions)

**prescriptions:** medication_name (VARCHAR, real drugs mapped to conditions — e.g. Pneumonia → Azithromycin 500mg, Cardiomegaly → Furosemide 40mg), duration_days (INTEGER, 1-365)

---

## Import Steps (Reproduce from Scratch)

Prerequisites: PostgreSQL 16 installed, Python 3.10+ with psycopg2

**Step 1:** Create database and run schema
```
psql -U postgres -c "CREATE DATABASE medical_rag;"
psql -U postgres -d medical_rag -f schema.sql
```

**Step 2:** Download `sample_labels.csv` from Kaggle (link above). Place it in the same folder as `seed_data.py`.

**Step 3:** Edit `DB_PASSWORD` in `seed_data.py` to your postgres password.

**Step 4:** Install dependency
```
pip install psycopg2-binary
```

**Step 5:** Run seed script
```
cd data/
python seed_data.py
```

**Step 6:** Run queries
```
psql -U postgres -d medical_rag -f queries.sql
```

All 10 queries run on a fresh database with zero errors.

---

## Queries (queries.sql)

| # | Type | Description |
|---|------|-------------|
| 1 | Aggregation | Count of diagnoses per disease type |
| 2 | Aggregation | Average patient age per department |
| 3 | Join (4 tables) | Full diagnostic history: patient → visit → scan → diagnosis → prescription |
| 4 | Join (3 tables) | Doctors and diagnosis counts grouped by department |
| 5 | Subquery | Patients with more diagnoses than average |
| 6 | Subquery | Scans with no diagnosis yet (pending review) |
| 7 | CTE | Patient diagnostic journey timeline |
| 8 | CTE | Monthly diagnosis trends per disease |
| 9 | Window (RANK) | Rank doctors by diagnoses within each department |
| 10 | Window (ROW_NUMBER) | Running total of visits per patient over time |

Coverage: 2 aggregations, 2 joins, 2 subqueries, 2 CTEs, 2 window functions

---
